name: getopt
readme:
Short:        Parse command options (OpenBSD)
Type:         util/cli
Architecture: m68k-amigaos >= 3.0
Uploader:     duncan (at) bowring (dot) us
Author:       Henry Spencer (ported by Duncan Bowring)
Version:      1.10

getopt - Parse command options (OpenBSD)

Original author: Henry Spencer
Ported by Duncan Bowring using amiport (AI-assisted porting toolkit).
Project: https://github.com/bdgscotland/amiport

Cross-compiled with m68k-amigaos-gcc for AmigaOS 3.x (68000+).
Tested in FS-UAE with AmigaOS 3.1.

Usage:
  getopt optstring parameters

  Parses command-line options for use in AmigaDOS shell scripts.
  Outputs normalized option list ending with --.

Examples:
  args=`getopt abo: $*`
  IF NOT WARN
    ; valid options
  ENDIF

  getopt "ab:c" -a -b arg file   ; outputs: -a -b arg -- file

Includes full source code (original POSIX and ported Amiga versions)
and PORT.md documenting every transformation applied.
porting_notes: Uses amiport getopt implementation (libnix getopt_long is broken, crash-patterns #17). Added __nowild=1 to suppress wildcard expansion of the option spec string. 40 tests cover short options, option arguments, -- terminator, invalid options, and error paths.
